sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Pre-eclampsia is a hypertensive disorder of pregnancy associated with maternal end-organ dysfunction and risk to both mother and fetus. Eclampsia is the occurrence of seizures in this setting and should be treated as a life-threatening obstetric emergency.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation
sections.content:
- Hypertension in pregnancy, especially if severe or rising rapidly
- Severe headache, visual disturbance, photophobia, or altered mental state
- Epigastric or right upper quadrant pain
- Nausea, vomiting, hyperreflexia, clonus, or seizure activity
- Dyspnoea, pulmonary oedema, or reduced fetal movement where reported

sections.title: Considerations
sections.content:
- Symptoms can precede seizure, stroke, intracranial haemorrhage, pulmonary oedema, or placental complications
- Pregnant patients can deteriorate rapidly and severe symptoms matter even before extreme blood pressure values are confirmed
- Any seizure in a pregnant or recently postpartum patient should raise concern for eclampsia until another cause is clear
- Left lateral positioning or uterine displacement may improve maternal perfusion in later pregnancy

sections.title: Management – Primary
sections.content:
- Primary Survey
- Treat seizures promptly under the seizure pathway if present
- Position to minimise aortocaval compression where appropriate
- Perform frequent blood pressure, conscious state, and respiratory reassessment
- Give oxygen only if clinically indicated
- Keep stimulation low where possible and prioritise urgent transport to an obstetric-capable facility

sections.title: Management – Advanced
sections.content:
- ECG
- Gain IV access early and monitor closely for deterioration
- Consider magnesium sulphate only within local protocol, scope, and senior support when eclampsia or severe pre-eclampsia is suspected
- Treat recurrent or ongoing seizure activity within protocol while maintaining airway and ventilation
- Consider antihypertensive therapy only within local protocol or senior direction
- Pre-notify receiving obstetric or emergency team early

sections.title: Additional Information
sections.content:
- Severe headache, visual symptoms, seizure, RUQ pain, pulmonary oedema, or marked hypertension should all increase urgency
- Eclamptic seizures may recur, so ongoing vigilance after apparent recovery is essential
- Record gestation if known, blood pressure trend, neurological symptoms, and seizure details
